CONTENT.Club career: Born in Gardone Val Trompia, Cittadini started playing at Lumezzane before joining Atalanta's youth sector in 2016, aged 14. Although he did not play consistently until he joined the under-17 team, he progressively came through the club's youth ranks, winning a national championship and two Super Cups with the under-19 team in 2020 and 2021.On 9 January 2022, Cittadini made his professional debut for Atalanta, coming in as a 90th-minute substitute in a 6–2 Serie A win against Udinese.On 7 July 2022, Cittadini joined newly-promoted Serie B club Modena on a season-long loan. Throughout the 2022–23 season, he established himself as a regular starter in the side, being praised for his league performances.On 4 July 2023, Cittadini was sent on a one-year loan to Monza ahead of the 2023–24 Serie A season.On 26 January 2024, Cittadini moved on a new loan to Genoa.
CONTENT.Style of play: Cittadini is a centre-back, who can play either in a back four or in a back three: he has been mainly regarded for his strength, his positioning and his ball-playing skills.
CONTENT.External links: Giorgio Cittadini at WorldFootball.netGiorgio Cittadini FIGC Italy U21 profile (in Italian)Giorgio Cittadini FIGC Italy U20 profile (in Italian)Giorgio Cittadini FIGC Italy U18 profile (in Italian)
CONTENT.International career: Cittadini made three appearances with the Italian under-18 national team in 2019 and 2020.In June 2022, he received his first call-up to the under-20 national team, subsequently making his debut with the Azzurrini on June 7, coming in as a substitute during the 1–0 home loss against Poland.On 22 September of the same year, he also made his debut with the under-21 national team, playing the second half of a friendly match against England.In December 2022, he was involved in a training camp led by the Italian senior national team's manager, Roberto Mancini, and aimed to the most promising national talents.

SUMMARY: Giorgio Cittadini (born 18 April 2002) is an Italian professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for Serie A club Genoa on loan from Atalanta.
